title: Server panics on startup: duplicate route registrations
evidence: main.go:295-299 registers /books twice (handleGetBooks, handleCreateBook) and /books/ three times (handleGetBook, handleUpdateBook, handleDeleteBook) on http.DefaultServeMux; running the binary crashes with 'panic: http: multiple registrations for /books' at main.go:297 (confirmed by executing ./bookapi).
suggestion: Register one handler per pattern that dispatches on r.Method internally (handlers already guard on method): http.HandleFunc("/books", store.handleBooks) and http.HandleFunc("/books/", store.handleBookByID).

title: POST /books unreachable as an HTTP endpoint
evidence: handleCreateBook (main.go:187) is correct and unit-tested, but its route registration (main.go:297) both panics at startup and is shadowed by handleGetBooks registered first on /books (main.go:295) — even without the panic, ServeMux keeps only the first handler per pattern, so POST would never reach the create handler.
suggestion: Dispatch POST inside a single /books handler based on r.Method.

title: PUT /books/{id} unreachable and never returns 404 for a missing id
evidence: handleUpdateBook (main.go:218) is shadowed on /books/ (main.go:296 registered first) and unreachable at runtime; additionally UpdateBook uses db.Exec (main.go:107) which never returns sql.ErrNoRows, so the err==sql.ErrNoRows 404 branch (main.go:248) is dead — updating a non-existent id returns 200.
suggestion: Dispatch PUT inside one /books/ handler; check RowsAffected()==0 to return 404.

title: DELETE /books/{id} unreachable and never returns 404 for a missing id
evidence: handleDeleteBook (main.go:259) is shadowed on /books/ (main.go:296) and unreachable at runtime; DeleteBook uses db.Exec (main.go:117) which never yields sql.ErrNoRows, so the 404 branch (main.go:273) is dead — deleting a non-existent id returns 200.
suggestion: Dispatch DELETE inside one /books/ handler; check RowsAffected()==0 to return 404.

title: Error responses are plain text, not JSON
evidence: Success paths use writeJSON (main.go:126) but every error path uses http.Error (main.go:134,164,170,177,179,189,195,201,205,...), which sends Content-Type text/plain; the spec asks for JSON responses with appropriate codes.
suggestion: Return errors via writeJSON with a JSON error body, e.g. {"error": ...}.

title: HTTP routing is untested — handlers invoked directly
evidence: main_test.go calls store.handleCreateBook/handleGetBook/... directly (e.g. main_test.go:57,175,230) and never exercises the DefaultServeMux, so the startup panic and shadowed routes escaped all tests despite test_coverage=0.552.
suggestion: Add an integration test that builds the real mux (or httptest.NewServer) and issues POST/PUT/DELETE over HTTP.

title: Fragile hand-rolled recursive contains() in tests
evidence: main_test.go:288-292 reimplements substring matching recursively instead of using strings.Contains; correct here but error-prone and non-idiomatic.
